description: Add run-scoped secret handling for factory execution so sandbox-backed runs can receive required credentials without persisting raw secret values in factory state, logs, timeline events, bootstrap records, sandbox output, or artifact metadata.

userStories.title: Define safe run secret schema
userStories.description: As a factory maintainer, I want run requests and records to model required secrets safely so that factory runs can describe credential requirements without storing raw values.
acceptanceCriteria: A factory secret input model exists for required environment variable secrets
Run records can persist safe secret metadata containing only name, source, required status, and redacted presence information
Serialized run records do not include raw secret values
Existing run record JSON remains backward compatible by adding only optional fields
Tests verify JSON serialization contains safe metadata and does not contain provided secret values
Tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Resolve required environment secrets during setup
userStories.description: As a factory user, I want missing required secrets to fail before execution so that setup errors are clear and no sandbox work starts with incomplete credentials.
acceptanceCriteria: Secret resolution happens during factory run setup before sandbox bootstrap or remote execution begins
Resolution supports required secrets backed by environment variables
Environment lookup is injectable for deterministic tests
Missing or empty required secrets return an actionable error that names the missing environment variable and does not include any secret value
Tests cover successful resolution and missing required secret failures
Tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Add token provider extension point
userStories.description: As a factory maintainer, I want a small token provider abstraction so that future short-lived credential sources can be added without redesigning the secret model.
acceptanceCriteria: A minimal interface or extension point exists for resolving non-env secret sources later
v1 behavior only implements env-backed resolution
Unsupported non-env sources fail with a clear setup error
The extension point does not introduce network calls or provider-specific dependencies
Tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Centralize factory secret redaction
userStories.description: As a factory maintainer, I want configured secret values redacted through one reusable path so that output and persisted state are consistently sanitized.
acceptanceCriteria: A redactor can be constructed from resolved run secret values
Redaction replaces configured raw secret values with a stable placeholder
Empty secret values are ignored and do not cause broad string replacement
Redaction handles repeated occurrences of the same secret value
Tests cover single-value, multi-value, repeated-value, and empty-value cases
Tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Redact factory output and timeline persistence
userStories.description: As a factory user, I want human output and factory event history to hide secrets so that credentials are not leaked through logs or stored timelines.
acceptanceCriteria: Human-facing factory output redacts configured secret values
Factory event and timeline persistence redacts configured secret values before serialization
Existing non-secret event fields remain unchanged
Tests verify persisted timeline and event data does not contain raw secret values
Tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Redact bootstrap command records
userStories.description: As a sandbox-backed factory user, I want bootstrap command records sanitized with run-scoped secrets so that setup logs cannot persist credentials.
acceptanceCriteria: Bootstrap command summaries are sanitized with configured secret values
Bootstrap command results and output metadata are sanitized with configured secret values
Existing bootstrap sanitizer behavior for env key names and configured secret values remains intact
Tests verify bootstrap persistence does not contain raw secret values
Tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Pass secrets to sandbox bootstrap execution
userStories.description: As a sandbox-backed factory user, I want resolved secrets available during sandbox bootstrap so that setup commands can use required credentials without persisting them.
acceptanceCriteria: Resolved secrets are passed to sandbox bootstrap through existing provider environment fields
Raw secret values are not written into run records, timeline events, command summaries, or artifacts
Missing secrets prevent bootstrap from starting
Tests use fake sandbox and provider dependencies to verify env propagation without real sandbox calls
Tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Pass secrets to sandbox remote execution
userStories.description: As a sandbox-backed factory user, I want resolved secrets available to the remote factory execution command so that the remote run has required credentials at execution time.
acceptanceCriteria: Resolved secrets are passed to sandbox remote execution through existing provider environment mechanisms
The canonical hal auto pipeline is unchanged except for receiving env already required by factory sandbox execution
Raw secret values are not included in generated provider command arguments
Tests verify remote execution receives expected env keys and values through fake provider execution
Tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Redact sandbox remote output and failures
userStories.description: As a factory user, I want sandbox remote output and failure summaries sanitized so that credentials are not leaked after remote execution.
acceptanceCriteria: Sandbox remote output timeline capture redacts configured secret values before user output and persistence
Sandbox remote execution failure summaries redact configured secret values before persistence
Tests verify remote output and failure summary strings do not contain raw secret values
Tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Redact factory artifact metadata
userStories.description: As a factory user, I want artifact metadata sanitized so that credentials are not leaked through artifact summaries or warnings.
acceptanceCriteria: Artifact summaries redact configured secret values before persistence
Artifact warnings redact configured secret values before persistence
Artifact payload storage behavior remains unchanged except for sanitized metadata
Tests verify artifact summary and warning strings do not contain raw secret values
Tests pass
Typecheck passes
